current_signals.name: contains_evidence_appendix
current_signals.semantics: binary check: Appendix present or absent based on heading detection
current_signals.gap: presence only; no row count, no dedupe check, no consistency

current_signals.name: evidence_quotes_with_citation
current_signals.semantics: count of `Evidence quote: "TEXT" — Company, jd_XXXXXX.` regex matches
current_signals.sufficient_for_new_contract: partial — provides total but not per-gap

current_signals.name: evidence_entries
current_signals.semantics: count of unique cited jd_ids after dedup on (jd_id, quote_text)
current_signals.ambiguity: differs from evidence_quotes_with_citation when a jd_id is quoted with two different spans — needed nuance for the new contract

current_signals.name: unique_cited_jd_count
current_signals.semantics: distinct jd_ids that appear in body citations

current_signals.name: quote_candidates
current_signals.semantics: any double-quoted span in report body
current_signals.ambiguity: includes prose quotes that are not cited-format

current_signals.name: fabricated_or_unmatched_quotes
current_signals.semantics: count of cited-format quotes that failed R1/R2/ellipsis tiers
current_signals.sufficient_for_new_contract: n/a — belongs to QI, not structural lint

current_signals.name: terminal_punctuation_only_matches
current_signals.semantics: count of R2 AMBER punct-only matches

current_signals.name: appendix_entries_not_cited
current_signals.semantics: count of Appendix jd_ids that no Evidence quote line cites
current_signals.sufficient_for_new_contract: partial — one of several body/Appendix consistency signals

current_signals.name: legacy_verdict
current_signals.semantics: green/amber/red aggregate over 25 structural checks + latency
current_signals.ambiguity: Appendix-missing currently red-level but check may not fail exit code even when Appendix absent (see 5e-implement-rejected runs)

current_signals.name: capture_scope
current_signals.semantics: which page section was captured (main section, fallback)
current_signals.sufficient_for_new_contract: gating — validator must consider truncated capture as not_evaluable

current_signals.name: fallback_used
current_signals.semantics: boolean · true when shortest-qualified-candidate fallback used

answers.question: Does the report contain the exact `## Evidence Appendix` heading?
Does the report contain at least 5 parser-recognized Evidence quote lines?
Does each of the 5 ranked gap sections contain at least one recognized Evidence quote line?
Does every cited body jd_id appear in the Appendix?
Are Appendix rows syntactically valid and parser-recognized?
Are duplicate Appendix rows correctly de-duplicated?
Are citations concentrated in one gap rather than distributed 5/5?
Is the captured report scope sufficient to make these determinations?

excludes: quote exactness (belongs to R1/R2 in QI checker)
whether five unique jd_ids exist (repeated jd_id is allowed if lines cover 5/5 gaps)

count_semantics.citation_line_count: count of parser-recognized `Evidence quote:` lines anywhere in body
count_semantics.per_gap_coverage_count: number of ranked gaps 1..5 with ≥ 1 recognized citation line
count_semantics.unique_cited_jd_count: distinct jd_ids across all body citations
count_semantics.appendix_row_count: distinct valid Appendix rows after dedup

policy.min_unique_cited_jd_count: no minimum (repetition allowed if lines cover 5/5)
policy.canonical_example_A: 5 citation lines · 5/5 gap coverage · 4 unique jd_ids · 4 Appendix rows — structurally VALID

candidate_strategies.description: Detect `## Your top 5 gaps` heading then numbered list
Extract shared pure helpers from prompts.ts or harness
candidate_strategies.reliability: n/a — current code has no such helpers
candidate_strategies.recommendation: do not refactor in this design loop

candidate_strategies.description: Regex: `^\d+\.\s+` at start of line inside the gap section; ends at next `##` heading

candidate_strategies.impl_size: small in code but adds dep
candidate_strategies.recommendation: reject — hard rule no new dependency without strong reason
reject alone; use as sanity check only

gap_parsing.recommended_rationale: no new dependency · deterministic · robust to prose changes as long as `## ` markdown-heading boundaries hold · lowest maintenance

appendix_parsing.existing_parser: extractAppendix in scripts/quote-integrity-check.mjs supports tab-separated + 2-space + pipe alternates

options.assessment: requires ESM import + refactor; not available today
requires spawning checker; adds latency + coupling; rejected
narrow deterministic parser mirroring extractAppendix's tab-separated primary path only (matches canonical prompt output); no dependency on other alternates

appendix_parsing.recommended_rationale: prompt now only offers one canonical format (tab-separated) · new validator can implement narrowly against that format · no risk of drifting from checker's more permissive parser · no coupling · easy to test
appendix_parsing.if_validator_and_checker_disagree: record both counts in structural_evidence_summary; a mismatch flags an amber structural_parser_ambiguity signal

deterministic_sets.body_cited_jd_ids: distinct jd_ids from recognized citation lines
deterministic_sets.appendix_jd_ids: distinct jd_ids from valid Appendix rows
deterministic_sets.duplicate_appendix_jd_ids: jd_ids appearing more than once in Appendix after row parsing

severity.duplicate_identical_appendix_row: AMBER (allow benign dedup miss; escalate to RED only if duplicate is a distinct row with same jd_id but different content)

GREEN: all body jd_ids represented in Appendix
repeated jd_id across DIFFERENT gaps is allowed and remains GREEN when all structural requirements pass (source diversity is out of scope)
AMBER: identical or clearly redundant citation line duplicated WITHIN the SAME gap
more than one citation in a single gap when the extra citation is redundant (all gaps still covered)
more than 5 total citation lines when the excess is redundant
structural parser ambiguity (validator vs checker disagreement)
RED: fewer than 5 ranked gaps OR more than 5 (contract says exactly 5)
any ranked gap lacks a citation
body citation jd_id missing from Appendix
malformed citation line where a citation was required
not_evaluable: capture_scope insufficient to see the gap section
capture_scope insufficient to see the Appendix

policy_selected.repeated_jd_id_across_different_gaps: GREEN when every gap has a valid parser-recognized citation, every citation is structurally valid, every body-cited jd_id appears in the Appendix, no malformed rows, and reuse is across different gaps · NOT a structural defect · source diversity is out of scope
policy_selected.more_than_5_citation_lines_when_redundan: AMBER (redundant excess only · non-redundant extras remain GREEN)
policy_selected.capture_insufficient: not_evaluable (do not overload RED with tool-side deficiencies)

cons: couples structural lint to QI R1/R2 evolution
risks touching R1/R2 tier logic accidentally
parser duplication (mitigated: prompt only emits one canonical format now)
implementation_options.regression_risk: low (does not touch any existing file)

cons: couples validator to harness release cadence
requires extracting stable helpers first (refactor risk)
implementation_options.recommendation: defer — worth revisiting only if a third checker emerges

exit_codes.0: GREEN or AMBER telemetry result generated successfully
exit_codes.2: tool / config / input error (unreadable file · malformed Markdown at fatal level · invalid args)

cli_contract.stdout: one-line summary (verdict + counts) for harness logs
cli_contract.on_malformed_markdown: exit 2 if unreadable · else best-effort with not_evaluable verdict
cli_contract.artifact_atomicity: write to output path via temp+rename to avoid partial artifacts

harness_integration.invocation_timing: after report capture · after QI check · before final verdict artifact

on_validator_error.action: record tool_error in metadata + emit structural_evidence_summary with verdict=not_evaluable and reason=tool_error
do_not: fall back to old behavior without a signal

baseline_metadata_design.first_implementation_baseline_mutation: NONE (do not migrate baseline metadata in the first implementation loop)
baseline_metadata_design.grandfathering_note: pre-validator baselines would require a narrow AgentOps-5e-followup-baseline-lint-migrate DECISION analogous to 5e-migrate if metadata migration is later approved
baseline_metadata_design.promotion_eligibility_first_loop: requires_review by default until validator has ≥ N controlled stability runs

test_matrix.scenario: 5 gaps · 1 citation each · 4 unique jd_ids (1 jd_id legitimately reused across two different gaps with distinct valid spans) — canonical Fixture A 5/5/4/4 pattern
test_matrix.note: repeated jd_id across DIFFERENT gaps is GREEN when all structural requirements pass; source diversity is out of scope

test_matrix.scenario: 5 gaps · 1 gap has 2 citations · all gaps covered
5 lines concentrated in fewer than 5 gaps
body citation jd_id missing from Appendix
malformed citation format for a required line
truncated capture missing Appendix section entirely
test_matrix.note: capture_evaluable = false — do NOT overload RED

test_matrix.scenario: identical / clearly redundant citation line duplicated WITHIN the SAME gap (structurally suspicious redundancy — NOT the same as ordinary reuse across different gaps, which is GREEN)
structural parser ambiguity (validator vs checker Appendix rows disagree)

rollout_phases.name: controlled A/B validation, no retries
rollout_phases.gate: separate TASK + RUN_REPORT + DECISION + human GO + cost approval

rollout_phases.name: baseline metadata migration, grandfathered
rollout_phases.gate: separate TASK + RUN_REPORT + DECISION + grandfathering exception clause

rollout_phases.name: separate promotion DECISION (structural lint → blocking)
rollout_phases.gate: separate promotion DECISION analogous to 5f-promote scope

Q1.question: Exactly 5 gaps or at least 5?
Q1.recommendation: exactly 5 — matches prompt contract 'exactly 5 numbered ranked gaps'

Q2.question: At least 5 citation lines or exactly 5?
Q2.recommendation: at least 5 — matches prompt wording 'at least 5 total'; > 5 is AMBER not RED

Q3.question: Is repeated jd_id across different gaps allowed?
Q3.recommendation: allowed AND GREEN when every gap has a valid parser-recognized citation, every citation is structurally valid, every body-cited jd_id appears in the Appendix, no malformed rows, and reuse is across DIFFERENT gaps · five unique jd_ids are NOT required · source diversity is explicitly out of scope · Fixture A 5/5/4/4 is canonical GREEN

Q4.question: Must every body-cited jd_id appear in Appendix?

Q5.question: Are uncited Appendix rows RED or AMBER?

Q6.question: Are duplicate Appendix rows RED or AMBER?
Q6.recommendation: AMBER for identical row dedup miss · RED only if same jd_id appears with conflicting company/title

Q7.question: Is more than one citation per gap allowed?
Q7.recommendation: allowed · AMBER ONLY when the extra citation is clearly redundant (e.g., identical line, or same-jd same-span duplicate); non-redundant additional citations remain GREEN; per-gap coverage 5/5 remains the hard requirement

Q8.question: Should missing Appendix be RED even while telemetry-only?
Q8.recommendation: yes RED verdict in structural_evidence_summary · but does NOT flip legacy exit code until a separate promotion DECISION

Q9.question: Should insufficient capture be RED or not_evaluable?
Q9.recommendation: not_evaluable — do NOT overload RED with tool-side deficiencies; harness completion_state already surfaces capture problems separately

Q10.question: Separate CLI or extend existing checker?

Q11.question: Should first implementation touch harness?
Q11.recommendation: NO — first implementation loop is standalone validator only; harness integration is phase 2

Q12.question: Should first implementation mutate baseline metadata?
Q12.recommendation: NO — baseline metadata migration is phase 4 under a narrow grandfathering exception

Q13.question: Should structural lint initially affect legacy verdict?
Q13.recommendation: NO — telemetry-only initially · matches QI arc precedent

Q14.question: Is a controlled A+B required before any blocking promotion?
Q14.recommendation: YES — phase 3 is a paid controlled A+B under separate DECISION; no promotion before it

Q15.question: Is 5f-promote authorized after implementation?
Q15.recommendation: NO — 5f-promote is a separate arc; structural lint stability is prerequisite but not sufficient

risks: narrow reimplementation of Appendix parser may drift from checker's more permissive parser — mitigated by validator-vs-checker AMBER signal
exactly-5-gaps invariant is prompt-imposed; if prompt shape ever changes, validator must update in lockstep
capture_scope not_evaluable path may mask real Appendix omission if harness capture is too narrow — mitigated by keeping harness completion_state independent
structural lint may raise its own AMBER signals for currently-benign patterns (uncited Appendix rows) — visible but non-blocking initially
any jd_id that appears both cited and uncited due to spacing/case bugs could confuse consistency checks — mitigated by exact jd_id string match
harness integration timing could hide new signal if verdict.md is inconsistent — mitigated by fixing verdict.md schema in phase 2 loop
baseline metadata migration cost is small but requires grandfathering exception per 5e-migrate precedent
one stability A+B is not sufficient to prove long-term probabilistic behavior — phase 5 needs multiple controlled loops
